_notes.loginLogo_appLogo: Both the login page's 'Swag Labs' title and the inventory header logo are rendered as plain text spans with no test id or unique class, so browser_generate_locator returned getByText('Swag Labs') for both. This text appears on every authenticated page header and the login page, so it is a weak/non-unique selector; consider scoping by parent (e.g. .login_logo or .app_logo class) in page objects.
_notes.cartLink_vs_cartBadge: The data-test='shopping-cart-link' attribute is on the cart-icon wrapper that also contains the badge; the badge itself only has the .shopping_cart_badge class (no data-test).
_notes.productNameBackpack: MCP generated locator('[data-test="item-4-title-link"]') — note this uses the product's internal id (4 = Backpack) which is stable across the demo. Generic text-based locator getByRole('link', { name: 'Sauce Labs Backpack' }) is an alternative but resolves to two links per card (image + title) and is non-unique without .first().
_notes.cancelButton_step1_step2: Both checkout step-one and step-two Cancel buttons share data-test='cancel'; they are scoped by URL/page in the tests.
_notes.removeBackpack_inventory_vs_cart: Same locator [data-test='remove-sauce-labs-backpack'] is used on both inventory and cart pages — each page only renders one instance.
_notes.inventoryContainer_ambiguity: SauceDemo renders TWO elements with id='inventory_container' — an outer wrapper and inner content div carrying [data-test='inventory-container']. Always use the data-test selector to avoid Playwright strict-mode rejection.
